task_completion.description: Navigate to the Library of Congress website and search for WPA National Park posters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to loc.gov showing search results or specific item pages for WPA National Park posters.

task_completion.description: Download 3 distinct high-resolution WPA National Park posters (TIFF or high-res JPEG).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser download manager or file explorer showing 3 distinct image files downloaded. File extensions should be .tif, .tiff, or .jpg, and file sizes should indicate high resolution (e.g., tens of megabytes for TIFFs).

task_completion.description: Save the images to ~/Documents/curated_images/ with descriptive filenames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager open to /home/ga/Documents/curated_images/ showing the 3 downloaded image files. The filenames must be descriptive (e.g., 'grand_canyon_poster.tif') rather than random alphanumeric strings.

task_completion.description: Create a credits.json file in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal showing a file named 'credits.json' located inside /home/ga/Documents/curated_images/.

task_completion.description: Ensure credits.json contains a list of objects for each image with the required keys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of credits.json. It must be a JSON array containing exactly 3 objects. Each object must include the keys: 'title', 'date', 'call_number', 'download_filename', and 'source_url'.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to genuinely navigate loc.gov and find the posters.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser (e.g., Firefox/Chromium) navigating loc.gov, rather than just creating dummy files via terminal.

integrity.description: The downloaded image files appear to be genuine high-resolution files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File sizes shown in the file manager are realistically large for high-res images (e.g., >20MB for TIFFs), or the images are visibly opened in an image viewer showing actual WPA posters.

integrity.description: The credits.json file contains actual loc.gov URLs and realistic metadata.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'source_url' fields in credits.json point to actual loc.gov domains, and 'call_number'/'title' fields contain realistic Library of Congress data, not placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory is /home/ga/Documents/curated_images/. The agent must download exactly 3 distinct images from loc.gov. High-resolution files from LOC are typically large TIFFs (32MB-164MB) or high-res JPEGs. The credits file must be named credits.json, located in the target directory, and contain a JSON list of objects with the exact keys: 'title', 'date', 'call_number', 'download_filename', and 'source_url'.
